mnemonics: CUSTER Model: Clustering Users' Search Terms for Efficient Retrieval. Think of General Custer organizing his troops into groups (clusters) to find the enemy (relevant documents).
keyPoints: The Cluster Model groups similar documents together into clusters based on their content.
A query is compared to cluster centroids (representative documents) first, rather than every single document, making retrieval more efficient.
It is a type of 'flat' clustering where documents belong to one cluster, improving search speed by narrowing the search space.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Cluster model with the Vector Space Model; the former groups documents, the latter represents them as vectors in a space.
Assuming a document can belong to multiple clusters; in a basic flat cluster model, it is typically assigned to just one.
